task_completion.description: A drawing or painting application is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A GUI window for a drawing application (e.g., KolourPaint, Pinta, GIMP) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: A blank canvas is visible within the drawing application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A clear, empty workspace or canvas area (typically white) is displayed in the center of the drawing application.

task_completion.description: A drawing tool such as a brush or pencil is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the application's toolbar, a brush, pencil, or similar freehand drawing tool is highlighted or visibly selected.

task_completion.description: Visible strokes are painted on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Lines, curves, or freehand marks of a contrasting color (e.g., black on white) are visible on the previously blank canvas.

task_completion.description: The painted strokes cover a noticeable area of the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The strokes are substantial enough to be easily seen, consisting of multiple lines or a large continuous stroke, rather than just a single tiny dot.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to paint, rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the cursor interacting with the drawing application's canvas and toolbars, not terminal windows executing ImageMagick or similar image-editing commands.

integrity.description: The agent painted the strokes manually rather than pasting an existing image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The canvas should show organic, tool-generated strokes rather than a perfectly rectangular pasted image or an imported graphic.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to write a script that modifies the image file programmatically.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) open with Python or Bash scripts designed to alter the canvas file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses a synthetically generated blank 800x600 pixel canvas. To pass, the agent must paint visible strokes with an intensity difference of at least 30 units from the background, a minimum stroke size of 50 pixels, and at least 1% total canvas coverage.
